metadata.name: InSynBio Fc Variants Atlas
metadata.description: Comprehensive reference of clinical and engineered Fc variants for half-life, effector, and bispecific engineering.

variants.name: LS (M428L/N434S)
variants.mechanism: Increases FcRn affinity at pH 6.0 ~10-fold by optimizing packing at the CH2-CH3 interface, enhancing IgG recycling without increasing pH 7.4 binding.

ip_status.expiry: US expired 2024; continuation claims may persist

variants.design_rationale: LS is the clinical gold standard for half-life extension due to its high efficacy (2-3x half-life extension) and low immunogenicity profile.

variants.name: YTE (M252Y/S254T/T256E)
variants.mechanism: Triple mutation at the CH2-CH3 interface. Dramatically increases FcRn affinity at pH 6.0 (~10-fold) but can slightly increase binding at pH 7.4.

receptors.FcgammaRs: Reduced ADCC reported in some contexts

ip_status.design_around: Core patents expired; YTE can be used for new designs.

variants.design_rationale: Provides greater half-life gain than LS in some primates, but triple mutation increases risk of sequence-based immunogenicity.

variants.name: LALA (L234A/L235A)
variants.mechanism: Disrupts the lower hinge region contacts with FcgammaRs (I, IIa, IIIa) and C1q. Reduces ADCC and CDC.
variants.design_rationale: Standard silencing variant when minimal effector function is desired. Freely usable.

variants.name: LALA-PG (L234A/L235A/P329G)
variants.mechanism: Combines LALA with P329G to completely abolish the 'proline sandwich' with FcgammaRs, eliminating residual effector function.

ip_status.design_around: LALA (slightly less silent but public), N297A (public)

variants.design_rationale: The most effective silencing variant currently in clinical use. P329G is the key differentiator.

variants.name: GASDALIE (G236A/S239D/A330L/I332E)
variants.mechanism: Introduces new electrostatic and hydrophobic contacts with FcgammaRIIIa, enhancing NK-cell mediated ADCC.
variants.design_rationale: Potent ADCC enhancement, but increased FcgammaRIIb binding can be a safety/efficacy trade-off.

variants.name: Afucosylation (FUT8-KO)
variants.mechanism: Removal of core fucose eliminates a steric clash with Trp158 of FcgammaRIIIa, increasing affinity 50-100x.

ip_status.design_around: FUT8 knockout is now more accessible; use Kifunensine in culture as a process workaround.

variants.design_rationale: Most clinically validated ADCC enhancement method. Cleanest receptor profile (selective for IIIa).

variants.name: HexaBody (E430G)
variants.mechanism: Promotes Fc-Fc hexamerization on the cell surface upon target binding, creating a high-avidity platform for C1q recruitment.
variants.design_rationale: Superior for targets requiring complement-mediated lysis (CDC). Surface-dependent safety mechanism.

variants.name: Knobs-into-Holes (KiH)
variants.mechanism: Steric complementarity in the CH3-CH3 interface drives heterodimerization of two different heavy chains.
variants.design_rationale: The foundation of bispecific IgG design. Simple, effective, and now public domain.

variants.name: SELF (S267E/L328F)
variants.mechanism: Selectively enhances affinity for the inhibitory receptor FcgammaRIIb while reducing activating receptor binding.
variants.design_rationale: Ideal for autoimmune applications where B-cell inhibition is the goal.

variants.name: N297Q (Aglycosylation)
variants.mechanism: Removes the conserved glycosylation site, causing CH2 domain collapse and total loss of FcgammaR binding.
variants.design_rationale: Cheaper manufacturing (can use E. coli), but Tm is reduced by ~6C. Good for ADCs.

variants.name: Xtend (T307Q/N434A)
variants.mechanism: Engineered for enhanced pH 6.0 binding to FcRn. Alternative to LS.
variants.design_rationale: High-potency half-life extension with distinct IP from LS/YTE.

variants.name: LALA-GN (L234A/L235A/N297G)
variants.mechanism: Combines hinge silencing (LALA) with aglycosylation (N297G) for extreme effector silence.
variants.design_rationale: Used when even trace effector activity (e.g. against T cells) must be avoided.

variants.name: DAPA (D265A/P329A)
variants.mechanism: Disrupts the upper CH2 region binding to FcgammaRs. Alternative to LALA.
variants.design_rationale: Often used in MedImmune/AZ pipeline antibodies.

variants.name: SDIE (S239D/I332E)
variants.mechanism: The core Xencor enhancement pair. Optimizes the electrostatic interface with FcgammaRIIIa.
variants.design_rationale: Proven clinical efficacy for enhancing ADCC in B-cell malignancies.

variants.name: D265A
variants.mechanism: Single point mutation that significantly reduces all FcgammaR binding. Found in many clinical candidates.
clinical_examples: Atezolizumab (uses N297Q, but D265A is similar)
variants.design_rationale: Simple, low-impact silencing.

variants.name: E345K (Complement Enhancement)
variants.mechanism: CH3 mutation that promotes Fc-Fc interaction, similar to E430G but at a different site.
variants.design_rationale: Often paired with E430G for maximum CDC.

variants.name: V262E/V264E
variants.mechanism: Favors FcgammaRIIb over FcgammaRIIa/IIIa.
variants.design_rationale: Early selectivity variant, superseded by SELF.

variants.name: S228P (IgG4 Hinge)
variants.mechanism: Stabilizes the IgG4 core hinge disulfide, preventing Fab arm exchange in vivo.
variants.design_rationale: Mandatory for all clinical IgG4 antibodies.

variants.name: E430G/S440Y
variants.mechanism: S440Y adds additional stabilizing contacts to the hexamer ring.
variants.design_rationale: Next-gen HexaBody optimization.

variants.name: DD/KK (Electrostatic Steering)
variants.mechanism: Complementary charge pairs in CH3 drive heterodimerization.
variants.design_rationale: Avoids bulky Knob/Hole mutations; good thermal stability.

variants.name: SEEDbody
variants.mechanism: Alternating strands of human IgG and IgA CH3 domains create asymmetrical interfaces that only heterodimerize.
variants.design_rationale: Very high purity (>99%) and stability.

variants.name: S298A/E333A/K334A (ALFA)
variants.mechanism: Triple mutation in CH2 that enhances FcgammaRIIIa binding. Early Genentech optimization.
clinical_examples: Obinutuzumab (Gazyva - though afucosylation is more prominent)
variants.design_rationale: Historical standard for ADCC enhancement.

variants.name: H435R (Staph A non-binding)
variants.mechanism: Abolishes binding to Protein A; used for purification of bispecifics or to modulate half-life in IgG3 contexts.
variants.design_rationale: Critical for multi-step Protein A purification of heterodimeric bispecifics.

variants.name: Recycling Ab (pH-dependent Ag binding)
mutations: None in Fc specifically, but paired with LS/YTE
variants.mechanism: Antigen-binding is pH-dependent (binds at 7.4, releases at 6.0). Paired with FcRn enhancement to allow one antibody to neutralize multiple antigens.
variants.design_rationale: The cutting edge of half-life engineering.

variants.name: L328W (FcgammaRIIb Selective)
variants.mechanism: Increases affinity for IIb while maintaining or reducing IIIa.
variants.design_rationale: Part of the SELF optimization path.
